title: Dhaniya Chutney (Coriander Chutney)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Fresh coriander leaves (dhania) – 2 cups, tightly packed
Garlic – 2 cloves (optional, but recommended for extra flavor)
Water – 2 to 3 tbsp (adjust for desired consistency)
Yogurt (optional) – 2 tbsp (for creaminess, can be added if desired)

instruction.title: Instructions
steps: Prepare coriander: Wash coriander leaves thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt. Roughly chop the leaves with stems.
Roast cumin seeds: Heat a small pan on medium flame. Dry roast cumin seeds until fragrant (about 1 minute), then let them cool.
Blend chutney: In a blender or food processor, add coriander leaves, green chilies, ginger, garlic, roasted cumin seeds, salt, sugar, and lemon juice.
Add water: Add 2 tbsp of water to start and blend to a smooth paste. Add more water little by little if needed to reach your desired consistency.
Optional yogurt: For a creamier chutney, add 2 tbsp plain yogurt and blend again until smooth.
Adjust seasoning: Taste and adjust salt, lemon juice, or green chili as per your preference.

chefTips: Serve fresh with samosas, pakoras, or any Indian snacks.
Use as a spread for sandwiches or wraps.
Pair with grilled meats or vegetables for a zesty kick.
